problem.title: Graph Valid Tree
problem.description: Given n nodes labeled from 0 to n-1 and a list of undirected edges (each edge is a pair of nodes), write a function to check whether these edges make up a valid tree.

example.explanation: A graph is a valid tree if it has n-1 edges and is fully connected with no cycles.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Union Find [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: A valid tree on n nodes must have exactly n-1 edges and be fully connected with no cycles. The edge count check eliminates most invalid inputs immediately, and then Union-Find with path compression and union-by-rank processes each edge: if both endpoints already share a root a cycle exists and the function returns false. The key insight is that exactly n-1 edges plus no cycle is both necessary and sufficient for a tree.
